chapter_title.ar: باب الرَّجُلِ يُقْتَلُ وَ لَهُ وَلِيَّانِ أَوْ أَكْثَرُ فَيَعْفُو أَحَدُهُمْ أَوْ يَقْبَلُ الدِّيَةَ وَ بَعْضٌ يُرِيدُ الْقَتْلَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 46 – The man is killed and for him are two guardians, or more, so one of them forgives or accepts the wergild, and one wants the (retaliatory) killing

text: رَجُلٍ قُتِلَ وَ لَهُ وَلِيَّانِ فَعَفَا أَحَدُهُمَا وَ أَبَى الْآخَرُ أَنْ يَعْفُوَ قَالَ إِنْ أَرَادَ الَّذِي لَمْ يَعْفُ أَنْ يَقْتُلَ قَتَلَ وَ رَدَّ نِصْفَ الدِّيَةِ عَلَى أَوْلِيَاءِ الْمَقْتُولِ الْمُقَادِ مِنْهُ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Hadeed and Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Jameel Bin Darraj, from one of his companions,

(It has been narrated) that it was asked from Amir Al Momineen<sup>asws</sup> regarding a man who was killed and for him were two guardians. So one of them forgave and the other one refused to forgive. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘If the one who did not forgive want him to be killed, he kills and returns half the wergild to the guardians of the killed one for the retaliation from it’.
